channel.title: Bugs on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Bugs on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: Why Troika Games Were So Buggy
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about why Troika games were so buggy.

I get asked this a lot and there are a lot of articles and videos talking about how Troika games, were just so incredibly buggy. And I do have a video on why RPGs in general are so buggy and I’ll link it in the description And I will re — remind you: Troika only RPGs. We made three RPGs. It’s not why we were called Troika: Um, but the short answer is we had a lot of feature ideas. We did not edit ourselves very much at all and we were a small team and that’s just combined to make very, very buggy RPGs.

item.title: Bugs In RPGs
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about bugs and particularly bugs and RPGs.

My experience is all in RPGs so I’m sure a lot of what I’m saying will generalize, but I just want to talk about why RPGs tend to be buggy, and this actually comes from a question a lot of people had questions. This is when I picked rrams 1989 asked: will all RPG games always be very buggy on release?

item.title: When Bugs Become Features
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to answer another question, this time from bite your own teeth, 2100.

He asks: Tim, are there any cases where you’ve embraced unexpected systemic reactions, or when has a bug become a feature? Oh, yes, uh, many times. I actually love when that happens. Um, first let me Define some stuff and then I’ll talk about some examples from my own games where this has happened, and then I’ll talk about why I like it so much. That’s a good question, because it got me all going.
